creator: edwin@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->open

> I dunno why you ignored my private e-mail about this PR so i repeat it 
> there. 

Please send me the message-id of the email and I'll see if I have 
received it. As far as I can see, I didn't receive it. 

> I think rrdtool should stay in the same category as mrtg, so proper 
> category for it is net-mgmt. 
> rrdtool is close to net-mgmt rather than database. 

As where MRTG was intended for all aspects of network device 
monitoring (polling of devices, storage of data, graphing of data), 

RRDTool is "nothing more" than a database layer and grapher of data. 
If you look at the programs supplied with it, it doesn't even have 
anything which can be used for gathering the information from remote 
devices. The only tool, related to RRDTool, which I know which has 
to live in net-mgmt is cacti, because that one is actually providing 
a network management feature. They even say of their product: "Cacti 
is a complete network graphing solution designed to harness the 
power of RRDTool's data storage and graphing functionality.". Nothing 
which states that RRDTool is anything but data storage and graphing. 

NetBSD has located it under databases instead of under net. 
Fedora Core has located it under databases instead of under net. 
Suse has located it under Science / Math instead of under Network. 
Ubuntu has located it under Utilities instead of under network. 

That's why I submitted this PR.
